mappings.rationaleAI: Confucianism addresses its history (B.1) including Confucius (B.1.b), the school (B.1.c), state orthodoxy (B.1.d), Neo-Confucianism (B.1.g), modern challenges (B.1.h), literature (B.2), and as religion/philosophy (B.3).
Chinese literary culture relates to religious symbolism (A.5), Confucian literature (B.2), and Taoist literature (C.2).
Modern China relates to effects of secularism on Chinese religion (A.2.d) and modern Confucian challenges (B.1.h).
Modern Japan relates to the Meiji era in Japanese religion (E.1.d).
Korean history relates to religions of Korea (D).
Samurai relate to medieval Japanese religion (E.1.b) and the Tokugawa era (E.1.c).
Japanese literature relates to Shinto literature (E.2.c).
Buddhism's East Asian spread relates to the Buddhist Way in Chinese religion (A.2.c).
Tibetan Buddhism's Chinese spread relates to Buddhist dominance (A.2.c).
Comparative mythology relates to Chinese mythology (A.6).
Silk Road facilitated Buddhist transmission to China (A.2.c).
Ritual relates to Chinese (A.4) and Shinto (E.2.e) practices.
Secularism relates to modern Chinese religion (A.2.d).
East Asian atheism question relates to modern Chinese religion (A.2.d).
